summary: A brief incident report about a Rackspace data center power failure that took 37signals offline. A traffic accident knocked out power to cooling systems, and when generators restored power, the cooling failed to restart. Machines were preemptively shut down to protect data, and everything was restored shortly after. The post serves as a transparent, real-time status update for customers.
key_points: A traffic accident caused a cascading failure in Rackspace's data center cooling systems
37signals preemptively shut down machines to protect data when cooling failed
No data was lost despite the extended outage
Multiple hosted applications beyond 37signals were affected by the same incident
money_quotes: No data has been harmed, the machines were preemptively shut down when the cooling systems failed.
key_insight: Transparent, real-time communication with customers during infrastructure outages builds trust, even when the cause is entirely outside your control.
